title: Jahmai Jones hits memorable first home run for Red Sox
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jahmai Jones hit his first Red Sox home run, a two-run shot over the Green Monster, against the Rays.
Jones joined the Boston Red Sox via trade and debuted this achievement during just his second plate appearance for the team.
Before joining Boston, Jones struggled with the Tigers, hitting .135 with 7 RBIs in the 2026 season.

title: Wilyer Abreu joins elite Red Sox company following his back-to-back multi-HR games
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Wilyer Abreu hit back-to-back multi-home run games, a first for a Red Sox player since Mookie Betts in 2016.
Abreu's performance at Fenway Park matched George Scott's 1971 feat of consecutive multi-HR games at the venue.
Abreu's four home runs helped push the Red Sox above .500 as they eye their 13th consecutive win.

title: Red Sox extend season-altering win streak to 12 games in epic comeback fashion
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox extend their win streak to 12 games with a comeback victory against the Tampa Bay Rays.
Wilyer Abreu hit his fourth home run in two games, helping the Red Sox rally from a 6-3 deficit.
The Red Sox hold the American League's final wild-card spot, leading the Seattle Mariners by 1.5 games.

title: Report: LeBron James ‘hates’ Celtics, would rank them last on his list of teams to join
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James reportedly dislikes the Boston Celtics, ranking them last on his list of potential teams to join.
NBA might delay releasing its 2026-27 schedule pending LeBron James's free agency decision, his fourth major move.
James is considering the Heat, Cavs, or Warriors for his final career move, ruling out a Boston Celtics stint.

title: Tom Brady busy at Fanatics Fest, from a face slap to showing off his arm
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tom Brady slapped Logan Paul during the Fanatics Fest panel on July 17, 2026, in New York City.
Logan Paul explained the slap was due to past rivalry after Paul's team beat Brady in a flag football game.
Brady also showcased his athleticism at Fanatics Fest, throwing a baseball and participating in other activities.

title: 4 charged in connection with alleged kidnapping, beating near Nantucket park
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four Nantucket men charged with kidnapping and assault after allegedly beating a 28-year-old man near Winter Park.
The victim was tied with an extension cord and sustained non-life-threatening injuries, including facial abrasions and bleeding.
Calray Brown, Dijon Hazel, Newton Lawrence, and Alvin Barnett released on bail, scheduled for arraignment Monday.

title: 2 arrested at Logan Airport as ICE activity ramps up
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Maria Rosales and David Ardila were arrested at Logan Airport amid increased ICE operations.
Rosales, under DACA protection since childhood, was detained due to a 2017 removal order.
Ardila, seeking asylum, overstayed his visa awaiting a decision and is under ICE detention.

title: 5 movies to watch after ‘The Odyssey’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Kevin Slane recommends five movies inspired by 'The Odyssey,' including Christopher Nolan's 'Interstellar' and David Lean's 'Lawrence of Arabia.'
The list also features 'O Brother, Where Art Thou?' by the Coen Brothers and John Ford's classic Western 'The Searchers.'
'The SpongeBob SquarePants Movie' is noted for its thematic elements resembling Homer's epic, despite its comedic nature.

title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox achieve an 11-game win streak, their longest since 2016, after sweeping Rays in doubleheader.
Game 1 concluded with a 10-0 win, highlighted by Jake Bennett's one-hit pitching over six innings.
Wilyer Abreu's two home runs in Game 2 contributed to a 5-3 victory, improving Boston's record to 48-48.

title: Chad Tracy offers updates on several injured Red Sox pitchers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ranger Suarez and Connelly Early are close to returning from injuries, with Suarez possibly starting on July 21.
Tanner Houck, Kutter Crawford, and Johan Oviedo are making progress in their recoveries and could return this season.
Roman Anthony, Garrett Crochet, and others remain shut down with no updates or timetables for their return.
